item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor edit:

The drone is allowed egress into the interior

Pretty sure that should be "ingress". "Gress" is a Latin root meaning "step"; "ingress" is literally "stepping IN", and "egress" is "stepping OUT". Like the difference between "immigrate" and "emigrate".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit I didn't get the reference when I first read it, but now I got it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You can check out any time you like… but you can never leave."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This falls too hard into the "MTF goes in and messed up shit" happens cliche, and like nico said, this goes on too long. The humanoid isn't compelling to me, and there isn't a clear arc or satisfying ending here.

I think this could be an interesting premise, but the execution doesn't work for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt as if this went on longer than it had to. The early logs didn't pull me in and not enough happened to keep me engaged with them throughout. Things didn't really get interesting until the very last log, with everything else before it feeling a bit generic. I also feel as though the metaphor could have been developed or hinted much better throughout the piece instead of just lore dumped at the end. All in all, this isn't a terrible piece, but it falls short of what I would want it to be for the above reasons.

Though I will say, the dialogue is pretty good throughout, and I for one am glad that an MTF doesn't immediately start cursing and screaming and pissing and shitting themselves when one of their fellows gets anomaly'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, thanks for your read and leaving feedback! My decision to word it that way was based on a desire to call attention to the word 'temporal' by itself, and then also to avoid using 'anomaly' to describe an anomaly. I can appreciate your position on the wording and I'll chew on your suggestion for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4709 is a temporal and spatial instability within 'The Grand Tahoe Resort & Hotel', near Tahoma, California.

I feel like you could just reword this to "SCP-4709 is an unstable spacetime anomaly" instead. It currently reads as off-putting and just plain weird to say that the anomaly is an "instability". That kind of description is pretty vague.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
4.0 star rating
5/11/2019

So I stayed here together with my partner and I gotta say it was pretty good. The service is nice and the decoration is just my style. The music in the elevator is a bit repetitive though and I stayed here together with my partner and I gotta say it was pretty good. The service is nice and the decoration is just my style. The music in the elevator is a bit repetitive though and I stayed here together with my partner and I gotta say it was pretty good. The service is nice and the decoration is just my style. The music in

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hello friends. I had a lot of fun writing this even though time loops hurt my head. A little surreal, a little goofy, but I think very 'me' if such a term can be used.

I'd like to thank Crashington for going above and beyond to more or less line-by-line this for me despite its dummy thicc length.
